text.title: Release Planning Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Release Planning. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Release has ONE named goal (verb + outcome): 'cut signup friction', not 'release v1.2'.
rule.rationale: Release has ONE named goal (verb + outcome): 'cut signup friction', not 'release v1.2'.

rule.statement: Readiness checklist (tests, docs, support, instrumentation) is green before ship.
rule.rationale: Readiness checklist (tests, docs, support, instrumentation) is green before ship.

rule.statement: Comms drafted + scheduled BEFORE ship; never after.
rule.rationale: Comms drafted + scheduled BEFORE ship; never after.

rule.statement: Rollback runbook has been dry-tested in staging within 7 days.
rule.rationale: Rollback runbook has been dry-tested in staging within 7 days.

rule.statement: 14-day stability window with error-rate + key-metric review before the release is closed.
rule.rationale: 14-day stability window with error-rate + key-metric review before the release is closed.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-release-planning.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
